expectations.text: Uses Enum or StrEnum for payment types (not magic strings)
expectations.evidence: response.md shows models.py with PaymentType(StrEnum), OrderStatus(StrEnum), Region(StrEnum).

expectations.text: Uses frozen dataclass for Order or result domain objects
expectations.evidence: response.md shows @dataclass(frozen=True) for LineItem, Order, ProcessedOrder domain objects.

expectations.text: Uses Protocol, singledispatch, or structural dispatch for different payment types
expectations.evidence: response.md shows payment.py using @singledispatch for payment type dispatch. match/case also used for status dispatch.
response.md shows errors.py with OrderError as base, PaymentError, InsufficientFunds, CardDeclined, PaymentTimeout.

expectations.text: Keeps operations as standalone functions rather than bloating a single class
expectations.evidence: response.md shows standalone functions in tax.py, payment.py, notifications.py, processor.py. No god-class.

claims.evidence: Old skill agent self-reported 7/9 patterns applied. Missing: context managers and functools.partial/cache (not domain-relevant). Protocol not applied for Notifier — old skill's Protocol guidance less prominent.

suggestions.reason: Old skill response did not use Protocol for the Notifier interface — used a plain class instead. The new skill's more prominent Protocol guidance (with before/after example) may help here.

eval_feedback.overall: Good output. Both versions handle this eval well. New skill's Protocol before/after example may improve Protocol adoption in future runs.
